symptoms.symptom_name: Abrupt-onset AND abrupt-offset ('switch-like'), regular, very rapid palpitations at rest

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Switch-like abrupt onset and offset with a perfectly regular very fast rate is characteristic of re-entrant paroxysmal SVT, and the recurrent pattern in a young heart raises accessory-pathway pre-excitation.
An instantaneous switch-like onset/offset with presyncope is atypical for a panic attack, which usually builds and eases gradually.
A perfectly regular rhythm argues against the irregularly irregular pulse of AF.

symptoms.symptom_name: Presyncope during the tachycardia (nearly fainted, had to lie flat) with chest tightness

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Presyncope reflects reduced cardiac output at very high rates and marks the arrhythmia as clinically significant, warranting a priority ECG.

symptoms.symptom_name: Recurrent self-terminating episodes (three this year) in a young, structurally-normal, well person with good exercise tolerance

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A recurrent, self-terminating paroxysmal pattern in a young structurally-normal heart is characteristic of re-entrant SVT and raises accessory-pathway pre-excitation.

symptoms.symptom_name: Episode fully self-resolved; no true syncope and no severe crushing chest pain (currently stable, in sinus rhythm)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Self-termination with no true syncope or crushing chest pain and a regular rhythm indicates a currently stable state suitable for priority outpatient work-up rather than emergency termination - while a future non-terminating or irregular episode would change this.

symptoms.symptom_name: Use of energy drinks / pre-workout supplements and moderate caffeine intake

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Concentrated stimulants lower the arrhythmia threshold and can precipitate paroxysmal SVT episodes; a modifiable trigger.

symptoms.symptom_name: Short PR interval (<120 ms), delta wave and widened QRS on resting 12-lead ECG (pre-excitation / WPW pattern)

investigation_details.investigation_name: Priority resting 12-lead ECG (and ambulatory/event monitoring if resting ECG is normal)
investigation_details.expected_result: Short PR interval (<120 ms), a delta wave (slurred QRS upstroke) and a widened QRS confirming ventricular pre-excitation (WPW pattern); if the resting ECG is normal, ambulatory/event monitoring may be needed to capture an episode
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: The pre-excitation pattern is never captured, WPW is missed, and the patient is not referred for potentially curative accessory-pathway ablation - and remains at risk of a dangerous future episode.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A short PR interval with a delta wave and widened QRS on the 12-lead ECG is the pathognomonic, defining finding of ventricular pre-excitation (WPW).

symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Suspected Wolff-Parkinson-White syndrome / recurrent paroxysmal AVRT
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Switch-like abrupt onset/offset regular tachycardia with presyncope, recurring in a young structurally-normal person, strongly indicates re-entrant paroxysmal SVT with suspected accessory-pathway pre-excitation - arrange a priority resting ECG and EP referral, coach a vagal manoeuvre for the future, and safety-net a dangerous recurrence.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Looks for the pre-excitation pattern (short PR, delta wave, widened QRS); should be obtained urgently (same-day/next-morning) even though the patient is currently well.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Ambulatory/event monitoring (Holter or patch) if resting ECG is normal
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Concealed or intermittent pre-excitation, or capturing an episode, may require ambulatory monitoring if the resting ECG does not show the pattern.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Cardiology / electrophysiology study and ablation referral
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: For definitive diagnosis, risk stratification of the accessory pathway, and potentially curative catheter ablation of recurrent AVRT.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Reassurance that the episodes are benign anxiety with no ECG or referral
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Dismissing recurrent switch-like presyncopal palpitations without an ECG risks missing pre-excitation (WPW) and a dangerous future arrhythmia.
